transitions.notes: The model never reads ontic state directly; it only captures observations or justified inferences.
Only admitted evidence may mutate canonical relationship state.
Direct evidentiary mutation of identity is exceptional and must be explicitly allowed by state_acceptance_rules.current.
Identity is normally derived from accepted relationship state plus admitted findings.
Applied control is recomputed when accepted identity changes materially.
Control findings may introduce newly admitted controller, actuator, or subordinate relationships.
Schema fidelity is reviewed against the accepted model, not ontic state directly.
Fidelity review is a governance overlay fed by accepted identity and applied control, not the terminal layer of plant state.

forbidden_transitions.reason: The model cannot bypass evidentiary capture and acceptance.
Relationships cannot be written as world-truth assertions without evidence.
Promotion review must not block evidence ingestion.

notes: These are knowledge and supervisory transitions, not entity lifecycle transitions.
Operational lifecycle transitions are defined separately in operational_transition_model.current.json.
fidelity_review_state is a supervisory governance overlay, not part of the epistemic stratum chain.
